employment.source: BLS QCEW annual averages, NAICS 518210 (Data Processing, Hosting), GA (area 13000)

industry_gdp.source: BEA Regional SAGDP2N — Information sector, GA (caveat: broader than data centers)

wages.source: BLS QCEW avg weekly wage, NAICS 518210 vs all-private GA

state_comparison.source: BEA SAGDP2N — Information sector by state

mw_peer_states.source: Compiled from public industry reports (CBRE/JLL/Cushman press releases). Directional only — methodology varies.

load_forecast.source: GA PSC docket filings (Tavily) + Georgia Power 2023 IRP + 2025 update (seeded curve)

water.source: GA EPD water-withdrawal permits + MW-based modeled remainder

policy.source: GA legis.ga.gov + AJC + Georgia Recorder (Tavily); exemption timeline curated.

pending_announcements.source: Tavily → DECD / gov.georgia.gov / AJC / DCD / DCF; advisory hints

annotations.label: GA data-center sales tax exemption enacted

headline.text: The 2025 Integrated Resource Plan, approved by the Georgia Public Service Commission, projects roughly 8,500 MW of total load growth over the next six years, with about 2,600 MW of that increase expected by the end of 2030; for the 2025‑2026 winter period the risk‑adjusted forecast adds roughly 1,200 MW of load, driven largely by large‑load customers such as data centers, which are anticipated to contribute about 3 GW of new demand by 2026, and Georgia Power has already filed more than 3 GW of new large‑load contracts, many tied to data‑center projects, underscoring the significance of data‑ce

permit_sources.snippet: Jeffrey W. Cown, Director Watershed Protection Branch 2 Martin Luther King, Jr. Drive Suite 1470A, East Tower Atlanta, Georgia 30334 404-463-1511 Notice of Draf
In addition, DESC has a NPDES10 permit (No. GA0003786) for the Stevens Creek Project from the Georgia Department of Natural Resources, Environmental Protection

water.methodology_note: Measured: GA EPD water-withdrawal permits (Tavily-extracted). Modeled remainder: operating MW not associated with permits, at 50% utilization × industry-standard 0.5 gallons cooling water per kWh. Estimate, not measurement.

exemption_timeline.event: Original data center sales-tax exemption enacted (HB 696)
exemption_timeline.detail: Exemption on equipment and electricity for high-tech data centers above investment + jobs thresholds. Authorized through 2028.

exemption_timeline.event: HB 1192 passes legislature; Gov. Kemp vetoes
exemption_timeline.detail: Bill would have paused new exemption certificates for 2 years pending a tax-policy study. Veto preserved the existing program.

exemption_timeline.event: Renewed legislative debate on exemption + grid cost recovery
exemption_timeline.detail: Bills introduced to require data center customers to bear grid-build costs directly. Status: under committee review.

active_bills.title: Data center grid cost allocation
active_bills.summary: Would require Georgia Power to allocate transmission and generation buildout costs proportionally to large-load data center customers.

active_bills.title: Data center water reporting transparency
active_bills.summary: Would mandate annual public reporting of withdrawal and consumption by facilities >5 MW critical IT load.

source_summary: Live sources updated 2026-05-30: employment, wages, establishments, facilities, counties, load_forecast, water, policy, pending_announcements. Stale or static: industry_gdp, state_comparison, mw_peer_states.
